title: Word: Styles & Templates
subtitle: Design Once, Use Everywhere!
overview: This session teaches students how to use Styles and Templates in Microsoft Word to create professional, consistent, and beautifully formatted documents. Students learn how Heading Styles, Paragraph Styles, and pre-built Templates save time and make documents look polished — just like real-world reports, letters, and newsletters.

images._note: Add screenshots and activity photos — Word Styles panel, before/after formatting comparisons, template gallery, student documents, table of contents generated from styles.

keyTopics: What are Styles in MS Word? — Pre-set formatting rules for text and paragraphs
Types of Styles — Character Styles, Paragraph Styles, Linked Styles
Built-in Styles — Normal, Heading 1, Heading 2, Heading 3, Title, Subtitle
Why Use Styles? — Consistency, speed, and professional appearance
Modifying Styles — Change once, update everywhere automatically
What are Templates? — Ready-made document designs you can reuse
Using Word's Built-in Templates — Resume, Newsletter, Report, Letter
Creating Your Own Custom Template — Save as .dotx file
Automatic Table of Contents from Heading Styles
Real-world Use — School reports, letters, newsletters, resumes

activity.title: Style It Up! — Format a School Report Using Styles & Templates
parts: Computer or laptop with MS Word or Word Online
Printed or digital plain-text report sample (no formatting)
steps: Open the plain-text school report provided by the teacher in MS Word
Apply 'Title' style to the document title using the Styles panel (Home tab)
Apply 'Heading 1' to each main section heading in the report
Apply 'Normal' style to all body paragraph text
Observe how the document instantly looks structured and professional
Now go to References tab → Table of Contents → Insert automatic TOC
See the Table of Contents appear based on your Heading Styles!
Challenge 1: Right-click any style → Modify → Change font or colour — watch all headings update at once
Challenge 2: File → New → Browse templates → Choose a Newsletter template and type your own content
Groups present their styled documents and compare — whose looks most professional?

quiz.q: What is a Style in MS Word?
options: A pre-set collection of formatting rules applied to text
quiz.explanation: A Style is a saved set of formatting rules (font, size, colour, spacing) that you apply to text with one click!

quiz.q: Which Style is used for the main title of a document?
quiz.explanation: The 'Title' style is designed for the main document title — large, bold, and eye-catching!

options: Main section headings in a document
quiz.explanation: Heading 1 is for top-level sections. Heading 2 goes inside Heading 1 sections, Heading 3 goes even deeper.

quiz.q: What is the biggest advantage of using Styles?
options: Changing one Style updates all matching text throughout the document instantly
quiz.explanation: Modify a Style once and every paragraph using that Style updates automatically — no manual changes needed!

quiz.q: Where is the Styles panel found in MS Word?
quiz.explanation: The Styles panel is in the Home tab — look for the row of style thumbnails (Normal, Heading 1, Heading 2...).

quiz.q: What is a Template in MS Word?
options: A pre-designed document layout you can reuse and fill with your own content
quiz.explanation: Templates are ready-made documents (resumes, newsletters, reports) with design, fonts, and layout already done!

quiz.q: How do you access built-in templates in MS Word?
quiz.explanation: Go to File → New to see hundreds of free built-in templates for resumes, letters, reports, flyers, and more!

quiz.q: What file extension is used for a Word Template?
quiz.explanation: .dotx is the Word Template format. Regular documents are .docx. Templates are saved as .dotx so they can be reused!

quiz.q: What amazing feature do Heading Styles unlock in MS Word?
quiz.explanation: Apply Heading Styles → go to References → Table of Contents → Word builds a TOC automatically from your headings!

quiz.q: A student applies Heading 1 to 5 section titles. She changes Heading 1 font to blue. How many titles change colour?
options: All 5 titles change to blue automatically
quiz.explanation: Modifying a Style updates ALL text using that Style at once — that's the power of Styles over manual formatting!

quiz.q: Which of these is a real-world document where Templates save time?
options: Writing a resume or job application letter
quiz.explanation: Resumes, cover letters, school reports, and newsletters are all made faster and better using Word Templates!

quiz.q: What is the 'Normal' style used for?
quiz.explanation: 'Normal' is the default style for body text — the plain paragraph style used for most content in a document.

quiz.q: Why should students use Heading Styles instead of just making text bold and big manually?
options: Because bold text is not allowed
Heading Styles enable TOC, navigation, and bulk updates — manual formatting cannot do this
Because it uses less ink when printing
Because manual formatting is too colourful
quiz.explanation: Manual bold/big text LOOKS like a heading but has no functionality. Heading Styles power the TOC, Navigation Pane, and bulk edits!

quiz.q: What does 'consistency' mean when using Styles in a document?
options: All pages have the same background color only
Every heading, paragraph, and title follows the same formatting rules throughout
The document has only one font size
quiz.explanation: Consistency means every Heading 1 looks identical, every paragraph is the same size — professional and easy to read!

quiz.q: What did students create in the 'Style It Up!' activity?
options: A professionally formatted school report using Styles and an automatic Table of Contents
quiz.explanation: Students applied Styles to a plain report, generated an automatic TOC, modified styles, and explored newsletter templates!
